channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Minnesota rolls out dashboard tracking federal funding cuts in the state
item.description: As of Wednesday, the site reported roughly $296 million in federal grants to Minnesota had been cut and millions more expected to be cancelled.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Public bathroom access in NYC to be voted on by City Council
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Knowing where the closest bathroom is in New York City can be a challenge, but a new City Council bill may change that. Councilmember Sandy Nurse is leading the push to expand public bathroom access.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Johnson vows to try again after GOP holdouts block action on Trump’s ‘big, beautiful’ budget bill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After abruptly halting votes, House Speaker Mike Johnson vowed to try again Thursday to approve a Republican budget framework, having worked into the night to satisfy GOP holdouts who refused to advance trillions of dollars in tax breaks without deeper spending cuts.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Free condoms, lube and sex ed materials in stock for all ages at CA libraries, thanks to Planned Parenthood
item.description: The Sacramento Public Library system adding condoms, lube and other sex resources in a dozen of its libraries in partnership with Planned Parenthood.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent denies that tariff pause is due to market declines
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent denied that the president's move to implement a blanket pause on his tariffs was the result of declines in the financial markets, arguing the action was part of Trump's strategy all along.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican-led House passes bill to limit nationwide orders from federal district judges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House passed legislation Wednesday mostly along party lines that limits the authority of federal district judges to issue nationwide orders, as Republicans react to several court rulings against the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's 'buy' tip on social media before his tariffs pause made money for investors who listened
item.description: President Donald Trump offered some financial advice on Wednesday morning that ended up making a lot of money for any lucky investor who listened to him.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump orders probe into 'Anonymous' New York Times editorial writer
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed a pair of memorandums directing the Department of Justice to investigate two individuals who served in his first administration and pushed back against Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kash Patel has been replaced by Army Secretary Driscoll as acting head of the ATF, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — FBI Director Kash Patel has been removed as the acting chief of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives and has been replaced with the Army secretary, three people familiar with the matter said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arvin approves special election for vacant city council seat
item.description: ARVIN, Calif. (KGET) -- The City of Arvin approved a special election to fill a vacant seat on the city council. The fully vote-by-mail election is scheduled for August. The decision was met with cheers from the public in attendance at Tuesday's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Treviño, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House votes to overturn Biden-era rule limiting bank overdraft fees to $5, sends to Trump to sign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The House voted Wednesday to overturn a rule that would have limited bank overdraft fees to $5, following the Senate in moving to dismantle the regulation that the Biden administration had estimated would save consumers billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: CORA LEWIS and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. stocks soar after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks surged on Wednesday after President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on the higher tariffs he had imposed on most countries — except for China.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam, MPR News

item.title: Federal judges in New York and Texas block Trump deportations after SCOTUS ruling
item.description: The Trump admin’s use of a wartime immigration law faces new hurdles as judges in two states block deportations, citing due process concerns and legal ambiguity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: New poll reveals Trump's approval ratings
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Army Secretary Dan Driscoll to lead ATF, replacing FBI Director Kash Patel
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel was replaced by U.S. Army Secretary Dan Driscoll to serve as the acting director of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ozempic and Wegovy: Sen. Fetterman pushes for Medicare to cover GLP-1s
item.description: Fetterman appealed to President Trump and Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. personally.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Real ID rules take effect in May; Minnesota officials say it’s time to apply
item.description: Starting May 7, travelers will need a Real ID or approved alternative to get through airport security. Officials say about 40 percent of Minnesotans have a Real ID. They’re encouraging everyone to get the necessary travel documents ahead of the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Stocks soar after Trump announces 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for some
item.description: Although markets opened mixed on Wednesday, stocks soared after President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Ag secretary: White House plan could help farmers hurt by tariffs
item.description: "The president has our farmers' and our ranchers' backs," Rollins said on Wednesday's "Morning in America.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: ICE to open office on Rikers Island with Mayor Eric Adams' permission
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – ICE is allowed to open an office inside New York City’s biggest jail, according to an executive order signed by Deputy Mayor Randy Mastro. The order stated that the safety of the city has been jeopardized by gangs like MS-13 and Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: Sen. Jim Justice 'really proud' of Trump for coal executive orders
item.description: The White House said the orders were enacted to "secure America’s economic prosperity and national security.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota pushes back on Trump demand to end DEI or lose school funding
item.description: Minnesota Education Commissioner Willie Jett told federal officials the state already complies with federal civil rights laws and that the Trump administration did not have the power “to unilaterally overrule the will of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk calls Navarro 'moron' after 'false' claims about Tesla
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk slammed President Trump's trade Peter Navarro on Tuesday, calling him "truly a moron.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. stocks are volatile as selloffs sweep through global markets
item.description: Shares nosedived around the world Monday as higher U.S. tariffs and a backlash from Beijing triggered massive sell-offs. Trump has defended the tariffs: “You have to take medicine to fix something.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman and John Ruwitch, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariffs pose danger for vulnerable Republicans
item.description: Vulnerable House Republicans are being put on defense amid the fallout from President Donald Trump’s sweeping global tariffs, which have stoked economic uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Despite backlash, Florida county term limit bill advances in the House
item.description: Florida voters may have the chance to set term limits, not just for state lawmakers, but also for county commissioners and school board members.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
